tips.title: Export any stay or trip as GPX
tips.description: On the Timeline page, right-click a stay or trip card and choose "Export as GPX". This is useful when you want to share a route, archive a segment, or inspect it in another mapping tool.

tips.title: Show favorites directly on the Timeline map
tips.description: Use the map control button "Show Favorites" on the Timeline page to display all your saved favorite points and favorite areas. It is a quick way to review known places while browsing daily movement.

tips.title: Choose the right favorite type: point or area
tips.description: Favorite points represent one precise place and match nearby GPS records within your stay detection radius. Favorite areas are map rectangles and match any records inside the zone, which is better for campuses, parks, malls, and other larger spaces.

tips.title: Manage favorites from Timeline or dedicated page
tips.description: You can create and update favorite places either from the Timeline map while you explore data, or from Favorites Management for bulk cleanup and structured editing.

tips.title: Track long-term milestones in Journey Insights
tips.description: Journey Insights summarizes your travel footprint with milestones and progress views. Use it to understand where you have been over time at country and city levels, not only day-to-day movement.

tips.title: Use Rewind for monthly and yearly heatmaps
tips.description: The Rewind page helps you analyze historical movement trends with detailed period views and heatmaps. It is ideal for spotting seasonal patterns and comparing different months or years.

tips.title: Monitor enter/leave events with Geofences
tips.description: Configure geofence rules to detect enter and leave events for yourself, and for friends who shared live-location permission with you. Use it for arrival alerts, routine monitoring, or automation hooks.

tips.title: Configure AI with your own key or server
tips.description: In Profile settings, you can configure an OpenAI-compatible key and endpoint, then use AI Chat to ask questions about your own timeline data and friend data when sharing permissions allow it.

tips.title: Drill down with Location Analytics
tips.description: Location Analytics lets you move from country-level to city-level and down to specific places. Use map and list views together to understand where time and movement concentrate.

tips.title: Tune Timeline behavior in preferences
tips.description: Adjust timeline detection and classification preferences to match your movement style and data quality. GeoPulse automatically re-generates timeline results after relevant preference changes.

tips.title: Share live location or timeline with guests
tips.description: Use Share Links to publish controlled links for live location and timeline history. These links can be shared with both authenticated users and guests, depending on your sharing setup.

tips.title: Override trip type when needed
tips.description: If GeoPulse classifies a movement incorrectly, open Timeline and manually override the trip type. Your correction helps keep analytics and reporting aligned with real behavior.

tips.title: Inspect trip classification reasoning
tips.description: Right-click a trip card in Timeline and open "Why this classification?" to review the decision process behind the selected movement type.

tips.title: Enable bicycle, running, and train detection
tips.description: Use Timeline Preferences to enable additional movement types and tune related thresholds. This improves classification quality for non-car travel patterns.

tips.title: Add friends and control what they can see
tips.description: From the Friends page, send requests and configure permissions separately for live location and timeline history. This keeps sharing flexible and privacy-aware.

tips.title: Promote your account to admin
tips.description: Set GEOPULSE_ADMIN_EMAIL in your .env to your user email, restart GeoPulse, then sign out and sign in again. After that, admin pages and system settings become available for your account.

tips.title: Control new user registration
tips.description: Admins can disable public registration in System Settings when they want an invite-only instance or tighter access control.

tips.title: Set global reverse geocoding providers
tips.description: Admins can configure primary and fallback reverse geocoding providers for the whole instance, including API keys and provider-level options.
